devanagari: श्रीकृष्णलीलानां संक्षेपतोऽवर्णनं तन्महिषीणां तस्मिन् । अनुरागाधिक्यं यदुवंशीयानामसख्येयत्व प्रतिपादनं च । श्रीशुक उवाच । सुखं स्वपुर्य...
english_devnagari: śrīkṛṣṇalīlānāṃ saṃkṣepato anurāgādhikyaṃ yaduvaṃśīyānāmasakhyeyatva pratipādanaṃ ca śrīśuka uvāca sukhaṃ svapuryāṃ nivasan dvārakāyāṃ śriyaḥ patiḥ sarvasampatsamṛddhāyāṃ juṣṭāyāṃ vṛṣṇipuṅgavaiḥ

translation: Trivakra’s son Upakrośa was exclusively devoted to Kṛṣṇa. He was a direct disciple of Nārada. He adopted the vow of life-long celibacy.

devanagari: स्त्रीभिश्चोत्तमवेषाभिः नवयौवनकान्तिभिः । कन्दुकादिभिर्हर्म्येषु क्रीडन्तीभिस्तडिद्द्युभिः ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: strībhiścottamaveṣābhiḥ navayauvanakāntibhiḥ kandukādibhirharmyeṣu krīḍantībhistaḍiddyubhiḥ
translation: The Sāttvata Tantra was composed by him. It has laid down Vaiṣṇavite purificatory rites for women, Śūdras and slaves (after which they are admitted to Vaiṣṇavism). By studying the Tantra, one attains Liberation from Saṃsāra.

devanagari: नित्यं सङ्कुलमार्गायां मदच्युद्भिर्मतङ्गजैः । स्वलङ्कृतैर्भटैरश्वै रथैश्च कनकोज्ज्वलैः ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: nityaṃ saṅkulamārgāyāṃ madacyudbhirmataṅgajaiḥ svalaṅkṛtairbhaṭairaśvai rathaiśca kanakojjvalaiḥ
translation: On the roads and streets, there was a heavy traffic—the highways being crowded with young elephants exuding ichor, well-adorned soldiers, richly caparisoned horses, chariots resplendent with burnished gold plates.

devanagari: उद्यानोपवनाढ्यायां पुष्पितद्रुमराजिषु । निर्विशद्भृङ्गविहगैः नादितायां समन्ततः ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: udyānopavanāḍhyāyāṃ puṣpitadrumarājiṣu nirviśadbhṛṅgavihagaiḥ nāditāyāṃ samantataḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ viś class 6 verb], [vocative single from √ viś class 6 verb], [accusative single from √ viś class 6 verb]

translation: It was richly surrounded with parks and pleasure gardens beautified with rows of blossoming trees which were resounded with the musical notes and warblings of birds and humming bees.

devanagari: रेमे षोडशसाहस्र पत्‍नीनां एकवल्लभः । तावद्विचित्ररूपोऽसौ तद्गेहेषु महर्द्धिषु ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: reme ṣoḍaśasāhasra pat‍nīnāṃ ekavallabhaḥ tāvadvicitrarūpo

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]

translation: Lord Kṛṣṇa who was the sole beloved consort of sixteen thousand queens, assumed as many various attractive forms as he had queens and severally sported with them in their separate resplendent palaces of surpassing affluence.

devanagari: प्रोत्फुल्लोत्पलकह्लार कुमुदाम्भोजरेणुभिः । वासितामलतोयेषु कूजद्द्विजकुलेषु च ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: protphullotpalakahlāra kumudāmbhojareṇubhiḥ vāsitāmalatoyeṣu kūjaddvijakuleṣu ca

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ kūj class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ kūj class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ kūj class 1 verb]

translation: The swimming pool in each of these mansions was full of crystal clear water scented with the pollen of full blown lotuses of various kinds such as Kalhāra, Kumuda and was loud with the warbling of birds.

devanagari: विजहार विगाह्याम्भो ह्रदिनीषु महोदयः । कुचकुङ्कुमलिप्ताङ्गः परिरब्धश्च योषिताम् ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: vijahāra vigāhyāmbho hradinīṣu mahodayaḥ kucakuṅkumaliptāṅgaḥ parirabdhaśca yoṣitām
translation: The sages were of different categories (observing different types of vows): Some subsisted on water, some on mere air; some abstained from food altogether but all of them were firm in the observance of vows, some were clad in bark garments and deer-skin, some grew matted hair, while others had clean-shaven heads.

devanagari: उपगीयमानो गन्धर्वैः मृदङ्गपणवानकान् । वादयद्भिर्मुदा वीणां सूतमागधवन्दिभिः ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: upagīyamāno gandharvaiḥ mṛdaṅgapaṇavānakān vādayadbhirmudā vīṇāṃ sūtamāgadhavandibhiḥ

translation: Some bathed thrice a day, some strictly observed the vow of silence, some were engaged in the study of the Vedas, while others in reciting praises and hymns to gods.

devanagari: सिच्यमानोऽच्युतस्ताभिः हसन्तीभिः स्म रेचकैः । प्रतिषिञ्चन् विचिक्रीडे यक्षीभिर्यक्षराडिव ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: sicyamāno pratiṣiñcan vicikrīḍe yakṣībhiryakṣarāḍiva
translation: Some devoted their minds to contemplation, some determined on meditative trance. Some had only skin and bones in their persons, a bundle of veins and arteries.

devanagari: ताः क्लिन्नवस्त्रविवृतोरुकुचप्रदेशाः । सिञ्चन्त्य उद्धृतबृहत्कवरप्रसूनाः । कान्तं स्म रेचकजिहीर्षययोपगुह्य । जातस्मरोत्स्मयलसद् वदना व...
english_devnagari: tāḥ klinnavastravivṛtorukucapradeśāḥ siñcantya uddhṛtabṛhatkavaraprasūnāḥ kāntaṃ sma recakajihīrṣayayopaguhya jātasmarotsmayalasad vadanā virejuḥ

translation: O king Parīkṣit! These serene-minded sages came from all directions. These meditators on Reality always waited upon Lord Kṛṣṇa, O emperor.

devanagari: कृष्णस्तु तत्स्तनविषत् जितकुङ्कुमस्रक् । क्रीडाभिषङ्गधुतकुन्तलवृन्दबन्धः । सिञ्चन् मुन्मुहुर्युवतिभिः प्रतिषिच्यमानो । रेमे करेणुभिरिव...
english_devnagari: kṛṣṇastu tatstanaviṣat jitakuṅkumasrak krīḍābhiṣaṅgadhutakuntalavṛndabandhaḥ siñcan munmuhuryuvatibhiḥ pratiṣicyamāno reme kareṇubhirivebhapatiḥ parītaḥ

translation: The city of Dvārakā was resounded with the sounds of Kṣattriyas of mighty prowess and of Brāhmaṇas whom penance was the wealth, of Sūtas and Māgadhas who were devoted to singing the glories (of the Lord) and by the auspicious sounding (playing) of musical instruments and by the loud recitation of the Vedas (by Brāhmaṇas).

devanagari: नटानां नर्तकीनां च गीतवाद्योपजीविनाम् । क्रीडालङ्कारवासांसि कृष्णोऽदात्तस्य च स्त्रियः ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: naṭānāṃ nartakīnāṃ ca gītavādyopajīvinām krīḍālaṅkāravāsāṃsi kṛṣṇo

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [imperfect active third single], [aorist active third single]

translation: The city of Dvārakā was resounded with the sounds of Kṣattriyas of mighty prowess and of Brāhmaṇas whom penance was the wealth, of Sūtas and Māgadhas who were devoted to singing the glories (of the Lord) and by the auspicious sounding (playing) of musical instruments and by the loud recitation of the Vedas (by Brāhmaṇas).

devanagari: कृष्णस्यैवं विहरतो गत्यालापेक्षितस्मितैः । नर्मक्ष्वेलिपरिष्वङ्गैः स्त्रीणां किल हृता धियः ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: kṛṣṇasyaivaṃ viharato gatyālāpekṣitasmitaiḥ narmakṣvelipariṣvaṅgaiḥ strīṇāṃ kila hṛtā dhiyaḥ

[nominative plural from √ hṛ class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ hṛ class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ hṛ class 1 verb]

translation: By virtue of the presence of the God of gods, Dvārakā became the most prominent of all sacred places and foremost among all holy places of (sacred waters) which wash off sins.

devanagari: ऊचुर्मुकुन्दैकधियो गिर उन्मत्तवज्जडम् । चिन्तयन्त्योऽरविन्दाक्षं तानि मे गदतः श्रृणु ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: ūcurmukundaikadhiyo gira unmattavajjaḍam cintayantyo
translation: So sang the queens of Kṛṣṇa as they being absorbed in him, remained speechless. While they were intensely thinking of the lotus-eyed Lord, they, sometimes, behaved like dullard and sometimes like a lunatic, uttered the following verses. Please listen.

devanagari: महिष्य ऊचुः । कुररि विलपसि त्वं वीतनिद्रा न शेषे । स्वपिति जगति रात्र्यामीश्वरो गुप्तबोधः । वयमिव सखि कच्चिद्गाढनिर्विद्धचेता । नलिननय...
english_devnagari: mahiṣya ūcuḥ kurari vilapasi tvaṃ vītanidrā na śeṣe svapiti jagati rātryāmīśvaro guptabodhaḥ vayamiva sakhi kaccidgāḍhanirviddhacetā nalinanayanahāsodāralīlekṣitena

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]

translation:
The queens said:
“O she-osprey! Lord Kṛṣṇa has covered his ever wakeful consciousness and has gone to sleep at night. Why don’t you sleep? Why have you lost your sleep and are wailing? O friend, has your heart too been pierced deeply by the noble sportive glances of the Lotus-eyed Lord, like us?

devanagari: नेत्रे निमीलयसि नक्तमदृष्टबन्धुः । त्वं रोरवीषि करुणं बत चक्रवाकि । दास्यं गत वयमिवाच्युतपादजुष्टां । किं वा स्रजं स्पृहयसे कबरेण वोढु...
english_devnagari: netre nimīlayasi naktamadṛṣṭabandhuḥ tvaṃ roravīṣi karuṇaṃ bata cakravāki dāsyaṃ gata vayamivācyutapādajuṣṭāṃ kiṃ vā srajaṃ spṛhayase kabareṇa voḍhum

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ naj class 6 verb], [accusative single from √ naj class 6 verb]
[nominative single from √ das ], [accusative single from √ das ]
[accusative single from √ juṣ class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ juṣ class 3 verb], [accusative single from √ juṣ class 6 verb]

translation: O Cakravākī (Red goose), at night you close your eyes but alas! you continuously lament so piteously! Is it because you cannot see your mate? Or are you a female slave like us of the Lord and desire to wear on your braids of hair the garland that rolls on (and is touched by) the feet of Lord Kṛṣṇa.

devanagari: भो भोः सदा निष्टनसे उदन्वन् । अलब्धनिद्रोऽधिगतप्रजागरः । किं वा मुकुन्दापहृतात्मलाञ्छनः । प्राप्तां दशां त्वं च गतो दुरत्ययाम् ॥ १७ ॥
english_devnagari: bho bhoḥ sadā niṣṭanase udanvan alabdhanidro kiṃ vā mukundāpahṛtātmalāñchanaḥ prāptāṃ daśāṃ tvaṃ ca gato duratyayām
translation: O ocean, why is it that you roar and thunder incessantly and do not get any sleep at all? Is it because you have been deprived of your beautifying ornaments like the gem Kaustubha and others! you have been reduced to our irrecoverable stage (who are deprived of our beautiful painting on our breasts, etc. by contact with Śrī Kṛṣṇa at the time of amorous sports).

devanagari: त्वं यक्ष्मणा बलवतासि गृहीत इन्दो । क्षीणस्तमो न निजदीधितिभिः क्षिणोषि । कच्चिन् गकुन्दगदितानि यथा वयं त्वं । विस्मृत्य भोः स्थगितगीरु...
english_devnagari: tvaṃ yakṣmaṇā balavatāsi gṛhīta indo kṣīṇastamo na nijadīdhitibhiḥ kṣiṇoṣi kaccin gakundagaditāni yathā vayaṃ tvaṃ vismṛtya bhoḥ sthagitagīrupalakṣyase naḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ kṣī class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ kṣī class 5 verb], [nominative single from √ kṣī class 9 verb]
[nominative plural from √ gad ], [vocative plural from √ gad ], [accusative plural from √ gad ]

translation: O moon, you have grown emaciated as you are a victim to the incurable disease of consumption. Hence, you are incapable of dispelling darkness with your rays. Is it due to your loss of memory, like us about the sweet secret talks indulged in by Lord Mukunḍa (Kṛṣṇa) that you have lost your power of expression? At least you look like that to us. (We think it that way).

devanagari: किं न्वाचरितमस्माभिः मलयानिल तेऽप्रियम् । गोविन्दापाङ्गनिर्भिन्ने हृदीरयसि नः स्मरम् ॥ १९ ॥
english_devnagari: kiṃ nvācaritamasmābhiḥ malayānila te govindāpāṅganirbhinne hṛdīrayasi naḥ smaram
translation: O Southern wind from mountain Malaya, what act unpleasant to you has been committed by us that you provoke the passion of love in our heart which is already torn and tattered by the side-glances of Śrī Kṛṣṇa?

devanagari: मेघ श्रीमन् त्वमसि दयितो यादवेन्द्रस्य नूनं । श्रीवत्साङ्कं वयमिव भवान् ध्यायति प्रेमबद्धः । अत्युत्कण्ठः शबलहृदयोऽस्मद्विधो बाष्पधारा...
english_devnagari: megha śrīman tvamasi dayito yādavendrasya nūnaṃ śrīvatsāṅkaṃ vayamiva bhavān dhyāyati premabaddhaḥ atyutkaṇṭhaḥ śabalahṛdayo smṛtvā smṛtvā visṛjasi muhurduḥkhadastatprasaṅgaḥ
translation: O beautiful cloud, (due to your similarity to Kṛṣṇa in complexion and his quality of removing the sufferings of the miserable—by showering your grace in the form of water) you are really a beloved friend of the Lord of Yādavas (Śrī Kṛṣṇa) who is adorned with Śrīvatsa. Hence you, being attached to him with bonds of love like us, are lost in his meditation. Your heart too must be agitated with keen anxiety and is excessively anguished for him. You are, therefore, constantly remembering him and shedding profuse tears like us. Contact with Śrī Kṛṣṇa is really very painful.

devanagari: प्रियरावपदानि भाषसे मृत । सञ्जीविकयानया गिरा । करवाणि किमद्य ते प्रियं । वद मे वल्गितकण्ठ कोकिल ॥ २१ ॥
english_devnagari: priyarāvapadāni bhāṣase mṛta sañjīvikayānayā girā karavāṇi kimadya te priyaṃ vada me valgitakaṇṭha kokila

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [vocative single from √ mṛ class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ mṛ class 6 verb]
[vocative single from √ mṛ class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ mṛ class 6 verb]

translation: O Cuckoo, blest with sweet notes! Your melodious cooiṇg is so sweet like nectar that it will infuse life into the dead. You imitate the sweet voice and utterances of our beloved Lord. Please tell me what pleasant services, I may do to you to-day.

devanagari: न चलसि न वदस्युदारबुद्धे । क्षितिधर चिन्तयसे महान्तमर्थम् । अपि बत वसुदेवनन्दनाङ्घ्रिं । वयमिव कामयसे स्तनैर्विधर्तुम् ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: na calasi na vadasyudārabuddhe kṣitidhara cintayase mahāntamartham api bata vasudevanandanāṅghriṃ vayamiva kāmayase stanairvidhartum
translation: O generous-hearted mountain, you are the supporter of the earth. You neither move nor speak anything. It appears that you are deeply absorbed in thinking about some great object (or project). Do you long to place the lotus-feet of the delight of Vasudeva (Śrī Kṛṣṇa) on your breasts (Breast-like peaks) as we pine to do?

devanagari: शुष्यद्ध्रदाः करशिता बत सिन्धुपत्‍न्यः । सम्प्रत्यपास्तकमलश्रिय इष्टभर्तुः । यद्वद् वयं मधुपतेः प्रणयावलोकम् । अप्राप्य मुष्टहृदयाः पु...
english_devnagari: śuṣyaddhradāḥ karaśitā bata sindhupat‍nyaḥ sampratyapāstakamalaśriya iṣṭabhartuḥ yadvad vayaṃ madhupateḥ praṇayāvalokam aprāpya muṣṭahṛdayāḥ purukarśitāḥ sma

translation: O rivers! O ye wives of the sea! (This being the summer) at present, your deep beds and pools are dried up; you have grown emaciated. Lotuses no longer beautify you. Just as we, being deprived of the amorous glances of our beloved consort, the Lord of Mathura, are robbed of our hearts and have become extremely lean and thin, you are denied the loveladen glances of your Lord in the form of rain showers (and are reduced to this plight).

devanagari: हंस स्वागतमास्यतां पिब पयो ब्रूह्यङ्ग शौरेः कथां । दूतं त्वां नु विदाम कच्चिदजितः स्वस्त्यास्त उक्तं पुरा । किं वा नश्चलसौहृदः स्मरति ...
english_devnagari: haṃsa svāgatamāsyatāṃ piba payo brūhyaṅga śaureḥ kathāṃ dūtaṃ tvāṃ nu vidāma kaccidajitaḥ svastyāsta uktaṃ purā kiṃ vā naścalasauhṛdaḥ smarati taṃ kasmādbhajāmo vayaṃ kṣaudrālāpaya kāmadaṃ śriyamṛte saivaikaniṣṭhā striyām

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [accusative single from √ vac class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ vac class 3 verb]
[nominative single from √ vac class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ vac class 2 verb], [nominative single from √ vac class 3 verb], [accusative single from √ vac class 3 verb]

translation: (By chance there arrived a swan. Regarding it to be an emissary of Kṛṣṇa, one of the queens addressed it). Welcome, O dear Swan! Be seated. Have some milk to drink. Tell us some news about Śrī Kṛṣṇa the descendant of Śūra. We look upon you as a messenger (between us and Śrī Kṛṣṇa). Is the invincible Lord hale and hearty? Does he of fickle friendship remember what he confided and professed to us in our privacy? (If you say that he has deputed you as he remembers us) O insignificant emissary (who come from a faithless lover) why should we resort to him? (If he invites us to make friends as you say) convey our message when Śrī (the goddess Lakṣmī) who cheating us all, monopolised his company is not with him. If he says, “How is it possible that she who is exclusively devoted to him can be avoided”, ask him “Among all women is Śrī the only woman who is devoted to him? Are we not so?”

devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाच । इतीदृशेन भावेन कृष्णे योगेश्वरेश्वरे । क्रियमाणेन माधव्यो लेभिरे परमां गतिम् ॥ २५ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīśuka uvāca itīdṛśena bhāvena kṛṣṇe yogeśvareśvare kriyamāṇena mādhavyo lebhire paramāṃ gatim

[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

[instrumental single from √ kṛ class 3 verb], [instrumental single from √ kṛ class 6 verb]

translation: It is by virtue of such deep constancy and devotion to Kṛṣṇa, the Lord of the masters of Yoga that the consorts of Kṛṣṇa attained to the Supreme State of Mokṣa.

devanagari: श्रुतमात्रोऽपि यः स्त्रीणां प्रसह्याकर्षते मनः । उरुगायोरुगीतो वा पश्यन्तीनां कुतः पुनः ॥ २६ ॥
english_devnagari: śrutamātro urugāyorugīto vā paśyantīnāṃ kutaḥ punaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ śru class 5 verb], [accusative single from √ śru class 5 verb]

translation: Kṛṣṇa’s great glory is widely sung in exquisite poetry and songs. Even if (Kṛṣṇa’s sports celebrated in songs are) heard, they irresistibly attract the minds of women. What need be said of those who had the privilege of seeing him directly?

devanagari: याः सम्पर्यचरन् प्रेम्णा पादसंवाहनादिभिः । जगद्गुरुं भर्तृबुद्ध्या तासां किं वर्ण्यते तपः ॥ २७ ॥
english_devnagari: yāḥ samparyacaran premṇā pādasaṃvāhanādibhiḥ jagadguruṃ bhartṛbuddhyā tāsāṃ kiṃ varṇyate tapaḥ
translation: How is it possible to evaluate the greatness of the penance of those women who had the privilege to regard the Preceptor of the world as their husband and render him loving service by shampooing (pressing) his feet (and such other personal attention).

devanagari: एवं वेदोदितं धर्मं अनुतिष्ठन् सतां गतिः । गृहं धर्मार्थकामानां मुहुश्चादर्शयत् पदम् ॥ २८ ॥
english_devnagari: evaṃ vedoditaṃ dharmaṃ anutiṣṭhan satāṃ gatiḥ gṛhaṃ dharmārthakāmānāṃ muhuścādarśayat padam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ sthā class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ sthā class 1 verb]

translation: By virtue of the presence of the God of gods, Dvārakā became the most prominent of all sacred places and foremost among all holy places of (sacred waters) which wash off sins.

devanagari: आस्थितस्य परं धर्मं कृष्णस्य गृहमेधिनाम् । आसन् षोडशसाहस्रं महिष्यश्च शताधिकम् ॥ २९ ॥
english_devnagari: āsthitasya paraṃ dharmaṃ kṛṣṇasya gṛhamedhinām āsan ṣoḍaśasāhasraṃ mahiṣyaśca śatādhikam
translation: Śrīkṛṣṇa who practised supremely ideal righteous way of life of a house-holder, had sixteen thousand and one hundred queens.

devanagari: तासां स्त्रीरत्‍नभूतानां अष्टौ याः प्रागुदाहृताः । रुक्मिणीप्रमुखा राजन् तत्पुत्राश्चानुपूर्वशः ॥ ३० ॥
english_devnagari: tāsāṃ strīrat‍nabhūtānāṃ aṣṭau yāḥ prāgudāhṛtāḥ rukmiṇīpramukhā rājan tatputrāścānupūrvaśaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative dual from √ aś class 5 verb], [vocative dual from √ aś class 5 verb], [accusative dual from √ aś class 5 verb]
[nominative single from √ rāj class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ rāj class 1 verb]

translation: Of those jewels of women, eight queens of whom Rukmiṇī was the foremost and (the names of) their sons have been already detailed to you in order of seniority, O king!

devanagari: एकैकस्यां दश दश कृष्णोऽजीजनदात्मजान् । यावत्य आत्मनो भार्या अमोघगतिरीश्वरः ॥ ३१ ॥
english_devnagari: ekaikasyāṃ daśa daśa kṛṣṇo yāvatya ātmano bhāryā amoghagatirīśvaraḥ
translation: Each of the (Sixteen thousand and one hundred) queens had ten sons from Kṛṣṇa. It means he had ten sons born from each queen. For the Lord’s course was never futile (VT. clarifies that no sexual contact of Kṛṣṇa was unfruitful).

devanagari: तेषां उद्दामवीर्याणां अष्टादश महारथाः । आसन्नुदारयशसः तेषां नामानि मे शृणु ॥ ३२ ॥
english_devnagari: teṣāṃ uddāmavīryāṇāṃ aṣṭādaśa mahārathāḥ āsannudārayaśasaḥ teṣāṃ nāmāni me śṛṇu
translation: Although all those sons were of mighty Prowess, eighteen out of them were great “chariot-warriors” who achieved noble renown. Listen to their names:

devanagari: प्रद्युम्नश्चानिरुद्धश्च दीप्तिमान् भानुरेव च । साम्बो मधुर्बृहद्भानुः चित्रभानुर्वृकोऽरुणः ॥ ३३ ॥
english_devnagari: pradyumnaścāniruddhaśca dīptimān bhānureva ca sāmbo madhurbṛhadbhānuḥ citrabhānurvṛko
translation: (They are as follows) “Pradyumna, Aniruddha, (Cāruḍeṣṇa) Dīptimān, Bhānu, Sāmba, Madhu, Bṛhadbhānu. Citrabhānu, Vṛka, Aruṇa, Puṣkara, Vedabāhu, Śrutadeva Sunandana, Citrabāhu, Virūpa, Kavi and Nyagrodha.

devanagari: पुष्करो वेदबाहुश्च श्रुतदेवः सुनन्दनः । चित्रबाहुर्विरूपश्च कविर्न्यग्रोध एव च ॥ ३४ ॥
english_devnagari: puṣkaro vedabāhuśca śrutadevaḥ sunandanaḥ citrabāhurvirūpaśca kavirnyagrodha eva ca
translation: The Sāttvata Tantra was composed by him. It has laid down Vaiṣṇavite purificatory rites for women, Śūdras and slaves (after which they are admitted to Vaiṣṇavism). By studying the Tantra, one attains Liberation from Saṃsāra.

devanagari: एतेषां अपि राजेन्द्र तनुजानां मधुद्विषः । प्रद्युम्न आसीत् प्रथमः पितृवद् रुक्मिणीसुतः ॥ ३५ ॥
english_devnagari: eteṣāṃ api rājendra tanujānāṃ madhudviṣaḥ pradyumna āsīt prathamaḥ pitṛvad rukmiṇīsutaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [aorist active third single], [injunctive active third single]

translation: Out of these sons of Kṛṣṇa (the enemy of Madhu), O Indra among kings, Pradyumna, the son of Rukmiṇī was the first and he was just like his father in all respects.

devanagari: स रुक्मिणो दुहितरं उपयेमे महारथः । तस्यां ततोऽनिरुद्धोऽभूत् नागायतबलान्वितः ॥ ३६ ॥
english_devnagari: sa rukmiṇo duhitaraṃ upayeme mahārathaḥ tasyāṃ tato
translation: That great (chariot) warrior married the daughter of Rukmī. He begat by her (a son named) Aniruddha who possessed the might of ten thousand elephants.

devanagari: स चापि रुक्मिणः पौत्रीं दौहित्रो जगृहे ततः । वज्रस्तस्याभवद् यस्तु मौषलादवशेषितः ॥ ३७ ॥
english_devnagari: sa cāpi rukmiṇaḥ pautrīṃ dauhitro jagṛhe tataḥ vajrastasyābhavad yastu mauṣalādavaśeṣitaḥ
translation: Aniruddha, himself a grandson of Rukmī through his daughter, married the grand-daughter of Rukmī (through his son). Vajra was born of her and only he survived the destruction from the pestle (in the civil war among the Yādavas).

devanagari: प्रतिबाहुरभूत्तस्मात् सुबाहुस्तस्य चात्मजः । सुबाहोः शान्तसेनोऽभूत् शतसेनस्तु तत्सुतः ॥ ३८ ॥
english_devnagari: pratibāhurabhūttasmāt subāhustasya cātmajaḥ subāhoḥ śāntaseno
translation: The name of Vajra’s son was Pratibāhu whose son was Subāhu. From Subāhu was born Śāntasena and he begat a son called Śatasena.

devanagari: न ह्येतस्मिन्कुले जाता अधना अबहुप्रजाः । अल्पायुषोऽल्पवीर्याश्च अब्रह्मण्याश्च जज्ञिरे ॥ ३९ ॥
english_devnagari: na hyetasminkule jātā adhanā abahuprajāḥ alpāyuṣo

translation: No king born in this family (of Śrī Kṛṣṇa) was destitute of wealth or less prolific in progeny or short-lived or weak or inimical to Brāhmaṇas.

devanagari: यदुवंशप्रसूतानां पुंसां विख्यातकर्मणाम् । सङ्ख्या न शक्यते कर्तुं अपि वर्षायुतैर्नृप ॥ ४० ॥
english_devnagari: yaduvaṃśaprasūtānāṃ puṃsāṃ vikhyātakarmaṇām saṅkhyā na śakyate kartuṃ api varṣāyutairnṛpa
translation: Even within the period of tens of thousand of years it is not possible to enumerate all those born in Yadu’s family who became famous for their great deeds.

devanagari: तिस्रः कोट्यः सहस्राणां अष्टाशीतिशतानि च । आसन्यदुकुलाचार्याः कुमाराणां इति श्रुतम् ॥ ४१ ॥
english_devnagari: tisraḥ koṭyaḥ sahasrāṇāṃ aṣṭāśītiśatāni ca āsanyadukulācāryāḥ kumārāṇāṃ iti śrutam

[nominative single from √ śru class 5 verb], [accusative single from √ śru class 5 verb]

translation: It is heard (by tradition) that there were three crores eighty eight hundred teachers for the children of Yadu’s family.

devanagari: सङ्ख्यानं यादवानां कः करिष्यति महात्मनाम् । यत्रायुतानां अयुत लक्षेणास्ते स आहुकः ॥ ४२ ॥
english_devnagari: saṅkhyānaṃ yādavānāṃ kaḥ kariṣyati mahātmanām yatrāyutānāṃ ayuta lakṣeṇāste sa āhukaḥ

translation: Who can enumerate the number of the noble-souled Yādavas? King Ugrasena was attended upon by billions of warriors (lit. ten thousand times ten thousand lakhs) of sons.

devanagari: देवासुराहवहता दैतेया ये सुदारुणाः । ते चोत्पन्ना मनुष्येषु प्रजा दृप्ता बबाधिरे ॥ ४३ ॥
english_devnagari: devāsurāhavahatā daiteyā ye sudāruṇāḥ te cotpannā manuṣyeṣu prajā dṛptā babādhire

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ han class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ han class 1 verb], [nominative plural from √ han class 2 verb], [vocative plural from √ han class 2 verb]

[nominative plural from √ han class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ han class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ han class 1 verb], [nominative plural from √ han class 2 verb], [vocative plural from √ han class 2 verb], [accusative plural from √ han class 2 verb]

translation: Those very wicked demons who were killed in the wars between gods and demons, were born again as human beings. They became arrogant and troubled the people.

devanagari: तन्निग्रहाय हरिणा प्रोक्ता देवा यदोः कुले । अवतीर्णाः कुलशतं तेषां एकाधिकं नृप ॥ ४४ ॥
english_devnagari: tannigrahāya hariṇā proktā devā yadoḥ kule avatīrṇāḥ kulaśataṃ teṣāṃ ekādhikaṃ nṛpa
translation: For their chastisement and control, gods were commanded by Hari and they descended in the race of Yadu. There were one hundred and one families of Yādavas.

devanagari: तेषां प्रमाणं भगवान् प्रभुत्वेनाभवद्धरिः । ये चानुवर्तिनस्तस्य ववृधुः सर्वयादवाः ॥ ४५ ॥
english_devnagari: teṣāṃ pramāṇaṃ bhagavān prabhutvenābhavaddhariḥ ye cānuvartinastasya vavṛdhuḥ sarvayādavāḥ

[perfect active first dual], [perfect active second plural]

[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: The glorious Lord Hari was the guide and controller of all the Yādavas. All the Yādavas who obeyed his command and followed him prospered in every respect

devanagari: शय्यासनाटनालाप क्रीडास्नानादिकर्मसु । न विदुः सन्तमात्मानं वृष्णयः कृष्णचेतसः ॥ ४६ ॥
english_devnagari: śayyāsanāṭanālāpa krīḍāsnānādikarmasu na viduḥ santamātmānaṃ vṛṣṇayaḥ kṛṣṇacetasaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ sam class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ sam class 1 verb]

translation:
[5]The Vṛṣṇis dedicated their minds to Śrī Kṛṣṇa so completely that they were not conscious of their own self while doing bodily actions like sleeping, sitting, wandering, speaking, playing, bathing and other (acts of daily routine).
[Additional verses in Padaratnāvalī’s text.]
47(A). With the sacrificial material produced by his Yoga- māyā, he performed sacrifices for twelve years at Kurukṣetra and thereby worshipped the Supreme Soul (which was identical with his self), the main substance or gist of all the Vedas.
purport:
Footnote [5]:
Bhāgavata Candrikā: Padaratnāvalī: Though they associated with Śrī Kṛṣṇa in activities such as sleeping, sitting, etc. with him, they did not realize that he was Lord Viṣṇu himself but thought him to be a prominent Yādava.

devanagari: तीर्थं चक्रे नृपोनं यदजनि यदुषु । स्वःसरित्पादशौचं । विद्विट्स्निग्धाः स्वरूपं ययुरजितपरा । श्रीर्यदर्थेऽन्ययत्‍नः । यन्नामामङ्गलघ्नं ...
english_devnagari: tīrthaṃ cakre nṛponaṃ yadajani yaduṣu svaḥsaritpādaśaucaṃ vidviṭsnigdhāḥ svarūpaṃ yayurajitaparā śrīryadarthe yannāmāmaṅgalaghnaṃ śrutamatha gaditaṃ yatkṛto gotradharmaḥ kṛṣṇasyaitanna citraṃ kṣitibharaharaṇaṃ kālacakrāyudhasya

translation: (That Śrī Kṛṣṇa’s renown is the holiest of all the holy places and Lord Kṛṣṇa is the best and most prominent of all gods is not wonderful). O King Parīkṣit, when the holy Lord (the holiest of the holy) incarnated in the family of Yadus, it made the holy water of the heavenly Gaṅgā that issues from the water (used for) washing his feet, inferior in holiness to his hallowing renown. (He is so great that) both who hated him or were affectionate to him attained to the same state of being similar to him. Goddess Lakṣmī for whose favour gods and others are striving, has devoutly dedicated herself to him exclusively. His name whether heard or uttered (even once) dispels everything inauspicious. By him were taught the duties to every gotra. He wields in his hand the discus (Suḍarśana) which symbolises the Time-spirit (which destroys all). Hence, it is not wonderful that he lightens the burden of the earth.

devanagari: जयति जननिवासो देवकीजन्मवादो । यदुवरपरिषत्स्वैर्दोर्भिरस्यन्नधर्मम् । स्थिरचरवृजिनघ्नः सुस्मितश्रीमुखेन । व्रजपुरवनितानां वर्धयन् कामदे...
english_devnagari: jayati jananivāso devakījanmavādo yaduvarapariṣatsvairdorbhirasyannadharmam sthiracaravṛjinaghnaḥ susmitaśrīmukhena vrajapuravanitānāṃ vardhayan kāmadevam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ vardh class 10 verb], [vocative single from √ vardh class 10 verb]
[nominative single from √ vṛdh ], [vocative single from √ vṛdh ]

translation:
Victorious is Lord Kṛṣṇa who is the sole refuge of all people (and abides in each of them as their indewelling soul). (As a matter of fact, he is eternal and birthless). That he incarnated himself from Devakī is a matter of (ignorant) talk only. Though he is attended upon by prominent Yādava warriors it is with (the might of) his arms that he annihilated all unrighteousness.[6] He completely destroys the sins of all—the mobile and immobile (e.g. grass, creepers, trees and other beings. With his resplendently beautiful countenance and winning smiles, he enhances love in the women of Vraja and the cities (and makes them attain to Mokṣa by subduing carnal love.
48(A). Padaratnāvalī’s Text adds:
A man should continuously meditate upon Lord Hari, the Supreme Soul, who is the creator, sustainer and the destroyer of the universe; who after creating the universe entered into it (as the Antaryāmin-indwelling soul), who assumed the form of a sage (Vyāsa) and composed this compilation of Purāṇas; having attained to him Śiva gets rid of the bonds of Prakṛti and like a bird in sleep, becomes free from the attachment to his body and property and remains in a state of blissful conscious existence, with no fear of returning to birth or Saṃsāra.
purport:
Footnote [6]:
Padaratnāvalī’s Text reads differently from this onwards:
With his arms and lotus-feet set on the earth for lightening the burden of the earth. His personality is the abode of Lakṣmī. By his charmṃg looks and smiling glances, he appeared like the God of Love to them.

devanagari: इत्थं परस्य निजवर्त्मरिरक्षयात्त । लीलातनोस्तदनुरूपविडम्बनानि । कर्माणि कर्मकषणानि यदूत्तमस्य । श्रूयादमुष्य पदयोरनुवृत्तिमिच्छन् ॥ ४९...
english_devnagari: itthaṃ parasya nijavartmarirakṣayātta līlātanostadanurūpaviḍambanāni karmāṇi karmakaṣaṇāni yadūttamasya śrūyādamuṣya padayoranuvṛttimicchan

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ iṣ class 6 verb], [vocative single from √ iṣ class 6 verb]

translation: He who is desirous of developing devotion to the (lotus) feet of Śrī Kṛṣṇa should listen to the stories of the sportive deeds of the Supreme Lord—the Lord who is transcendental to Prakṛti and who for the vindication of the path of righteousness ordained by himself, has assumed different sportive forms (such as the Fish, the Tortoise and others) and performed (miraculous) sportful arts suitable to that form and capable of destroying all Karmas.

devanagari: मर्त्यस्तयानुसवमेधितया मुकुन्द । श्रीमत्कथाश्रवणकीर्तनचिन्तयैति । तद्धाम दुस्तरकृतान्तजवापवर्गं । ग्रामाद्वनं क्षितिभुजोऽपि ययुर्यदर्थ...
english_devnagari: martyastayānusavamedhitayā mukunda śrīmatkathāśravaṇakīrtanacintayaiti taddhāma dustarakṛtāntajavāpavargaṃ grāmādvanaṃ kṣitibhujo

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [imperative active second single], [imperative middle first single]

translation:
By attentively listening to, reciting and constantly meditating on the glorious stories of Lord Mukunda, a man attains to his region which is beyond the jurisdiction of the god of Death (or the Time-spirit) of irresistible force and for the sake of attaining which even the rulers of the earth renounced their (comfortable) house-holder’s life (and their kingdoms) and betook themselves to the forest (and performed penance).
[Padaratnāvalī’s Text adds:]
50(A). He who devoutly and with faith listens to or narrates to others, the life and deeds of Lord Kṛṣṇa (as glorified by Śuka, the son of Vyāsa) of immortal and blissful glory which completely destroy the sin of the world and are a beautiful ornament to the ears of His devotees, can concentrate his mind on??e [the?] glorious Lord and ultimately attain to his blissful region where there is no fear from any quarter.
[back to top]

devanagari: इति श्रीमद्‍भागवते महापुराणे पारमहंस्यां । संहितायां दशमस्कन्धे उत्तरार्धे । श्रीकृष्णचरितानुवर्णनं नाम नवतितमोऽध्यायः ॥ ९० ॥
english_devnagari: iti śrīmad‍bhāgavate mahāpurāṇe pāramahaṃsyāṃ saṃhitāyāṃ daśamaskandhe uttarārdhe śrīkṛṣṇacaritānuvarṇanaṃ nāma navatitamo
